NL.R1: In order that access to an IT system is reliably prevented during a short absence of the IT user, it should only be possible to disable a screen lock after successful user authentication
NL.R2: It should be possible for the user to activate the screen lock manually
NL.R3: In addition, the screen lock should be automatically initiated after a predefined period of inactivity

Humanmade.R3: exists('automatic screen lock') and timed_alternative('automatic screen lock', 'user input', 60')

Generated2.R2: exists(tree, 'activate screen lock manually') and executed_by(tree, 'activate screen lock manually', 'user')

Generated3.R2: exists(tree, 'Activate Screen Lock Manually') and executed_by(tree, 'Activate Screen Lock Manually', 'IT User')

Generated4.R1: precedence(tree, 'Disable Screen Lock', 'Authenticate User') and data_leads_to_absence(tree, 'not authenticationSuccess', 'Disable Screen Lock')

Generated5.R3: timed_alternative(tree, 'Perform user activity', 'Initiate screen lock automatically', 300)

Generated6.R3: timed_alternative(tree, 'Perform User Activity', 'Initiate Screen Lock Automatically', 300)

Generated8.R3: timed_alternative(tree, 'perform IT activity', 'initiate screen lock automatically', 600)
